intro: Mayor Bill de Blasio created the Department of Veterans' Services on April 8, 2016, making it the first standalone city agency in the country dedicated exclusively to serving veterans and their families. DVS connects New York City's veteran community — including caregivers, survivors, and families of past and present service members — to services in economic empowerment, housing security, benefits, and health and wellness, through Veteran Resource Centers in each of the five boroughs.

cardHighlights.function: Connects veterans and families to housing, benefits, and health services
cardHighlights.scale: Serves an estimated 210,000 veterans and service members citywide
